title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: unifyr provides tools auditing, validating, preparing panel datasets statistical analysis. Panel datasets often contain duplicate unit-time observations, missing time periods, irregular gaps, imbalance. issues can affect fixed effects models, difference--differences designs, event studies, panel-data methods. goal unifyr help users identify issues estimation.

title: Getting Started with unifyr
code: library(unifyr)

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: unifyr includes small example dataset called example_panel. dataset intentionally includes: duplicate unit-time observation missing unit-time cells unbalanced panel structure makes useful demonstrating panel-data diagnostics.

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: main function audit_panel(). gives quick overview panel structure, including whether panel balanced whether missing duplicate unit-time cells.
code: audit_panel(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> Panel audit #>  #> Data: example_panel #> Unit variable: id #> Time variable: year #>  #> Units: 3 #> Time periods: 4 #> Observed rows: 9 #> Observed id-time cells: 8 #> Expected id-time cells: 12 #> Missing id-time cells: 4 #> Duplicate id-time cells: 1 #> Balanced panel: No

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: Duplicate unit-time observations common problem panel datasets.
code: duplicate_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id unifyr_duplicate_cells unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows #>   <dbl>                  <int>                       <int> #> 1     1                      1                           1

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: gap_summary() identifies missing time periods panel unit.
code: gap_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      id unifyr_missing_periods #>   <dbl>                  <int> #> 1     1                      2 #> 2     2                      2

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: flag_panel_issues() adds diagnostic flags data.
code: flag_panel_issues(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 9 × 7 #>      id  year outcome treatment unifyr_row_id unifyr_id_time_n #>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl>         <int>            <int> #> 1     1  2020      10         0             1                1 #> 2     1  2021      12         1             2                2 #> 3     1  2021      13         1             3                2 #> 4     2  2020      20         0             4                1 #> 5     2  2022      25         1             5                1 #> 6     3  2020      30         0             6                1 #> 7     3  2021      31         0             7                1 #> 8     3  2022      32         1             8                1 #> 9     3  2023      33         1             9                1 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: unifyr_duplicate_cell <lgl>

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: complete_panel() creates complete unit-time grid. impute missing outcome values. complete_panel() requires unique unit-time cells, first remove duplicate id-time observations example dataset.
code: example_panel_unique <- example_panel |>   dplyr::distinct(id, year, .keep_all = TRUE)  complete_panel(example_panel_unique, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 12 × 7 #>       id  year outcome treatment unifyr_original_row unifyr_completed_cell #>    <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> <lgl>               <lgl>                 #>  1     1  2020      10         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  2     1  2021      12         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  3     1  2022      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  4     1  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  5     2  2020      20         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  6     2  2021      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  7     2  2022      25         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  8     2  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  9     3  2020      30         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 10     3  2021      31         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 11     3  2022      32         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 12     3  2023      33         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: unifyr_audit_action <chr>

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: typical unifyr workflow :
code: library(unifyr)  audit_panel(my_data, id = unit_id, time = year)  duplicate_summary(my_data, id = unit_id, time = year)  gap_summary(my_data, id = unit_id, time = year)  clean_data <- my_data |>   dplyr::distinct(unit_id, year, .keep_all = TRUE)  complete_panel(clean_data, id = unit_id, time = year)

title: Getting Started with unifyr
text: unifyr designed provide transparent reproducible workflow panel-data quality assurance. Use fitting panel models, difference--differences designs, event studies, longitudinal-data analyses.

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: unifyr provides tools auditing, validating, preparing panel datasets statistical analysis.

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: can install development version unifyr GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("desirajulavanya/R-Package-Unify")

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: Panel datasets often contain missing unit-time cells, duplicate observations, irregular time gaps, imbalance. issues can affect fixed effects models, difference--differences designs, event studies, panel-data methods. unifyr helps researchers identify document problems estimation.

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
code: audit_panel(example_panel, id = id, time = year) ## Panel audit ##  ## Data: example_panel ## Unit variable: id ## Time variable: year ##  ## Units: 3 ## Time periods: 4 ## Observed rows: 9 ## Observed id-time cells: 8 ## Expected id-time cells: 12 ## Missing id-time cells: 4 ## Duplicate id-time cells: 1 ## Balanced panel: No

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
code: duplicate_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) ## # A tibble: 1 × 3 ##      id unifyr_duplicate_cells unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows ##   <dbl>                  <int>                       <int> ## 1     1                      1                           1

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
code: gap_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) ## # A tibble: 2 × 2 ##      id unifyr_missing_periods ##   <dbl>                  <int> ## 1     1                      2 ## 2     2                      2

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
code: flag_panel_issues(example_panel, id = id, time = year) ## # A tibble: 9 × 7 ##      id  year outcome treatment unifyr_row_id unifyr_id_time_n ##   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl>         <int>            <int> ## 1     1  2020      10         0             1                1 ## 2     1  2021      12         1             2                2 ## 3     1  2021      13         1             3                2 ## 4     2  2020      20         0             4                1 ## 5     2  2022      25         1             5                1 ## 6     3  2020      30         0             6                1 ## 7     3  2021      31         0             7                1 ## 8     3  2022      32         1             8                1 ## 9     3  2023      33         1             9                1 ## # ℹ 1 more variable: unifyr_duplicate_cell <lgl>

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: complete_panel() creates full unit-time grid preserving observed values. impute missing outcomes. complete_panel() requires unique id-time cells, first create version example data without duplicates.
code: example_panel_unique <- example_panel |>   dplyr::distinct(id, year, .keep_all = TRUE)  complete_panel(example_panel_unique, id = id, time = year) ## # A tibble: 12 × 7 ##       id  year outcome treatment unifyr_original_row unifyr_completed_cell ##    <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> <lgl>               <lgl>                 ##  1     1  2020      10         0 TRUE                FALSE                 ##  2     1  2021      12         1 TRUE                FALSE                 ##  3     1  2022      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  ##  4     1  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  ##  5     2  2020      20         0 TRUE                FALSE                 ##  6     2  2021      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  ##  7     2  2022      25         1 TRUE                FALSE                 ##  8     2  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  ##  9     3  2020      30         0 TRUE                FALSE                 ## 10     3  2021      31         0 TRUE                FALSE                 ## 11     3  2022      32         1 TRUE                FALSE                 ## 12     3  2023      33         1 TRUE                FALSE                 ## # ℹ 1 more variable: unifyr_audit_action <chr>

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: audit_panel() gives full panel diagnostic summary. duplicate_summary() finds duplicate unit-time observations. gap_summary() summarizes missing time periods unit. flag_panel_issues() flags row-level panel problems. complete_panel() creates complete panel grid without imputing observed variables.

title: unifyr: Panel Data Auditing Tools
text: goal unifyr provide transparent reproducible workflow panel-data quality assurance statistical modeling.

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
text: audit_panel() checks whether dataset expected structure panel dataset. reports number panel units, time periods, observed rows, unique unit-time cells, expected unit-time cells, missing unit-time cells, duplicate unit-time cells, whether panel balanced.

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
code: audit_panel(data, id, time)

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
text: data data frame tibble. id Unquoted column name identifying panel unit, person, firm, district, county, country. time Unquoted column name identifying time period, year, month, quarter, date.

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
text: object class unifyr_panel_audit. object list containing panel metadata, balance information, counts missing duplicate unit-time cells, data frames containing missing duplicate cells.

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
text: panel treated balanced every observed panel unit appears every observed time period exactly . Missing cells unit-time combinations implied full unit--time grid present data. Duplicate cells unit-time combinations appear . audit_panel() modify input data. returns audit object can summarized audit_summary() inspected accessor functions missing_cells() duplicate_cells().

title: Audit a panel dataset — audit_panel
code: audit_panel(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> Panel audit #>  #> Data: example_panel #> Unit variable: id #> Time variable: year #>  #> Units: 3 #> Time periods: 4 #> Observed rows: 9 #> Observed id-time cells: 8 #> Expected id-time cells: 12 #> Missing id-time cells: 4 #> Duplicate id-time cells: 1 #> Balanced panel: No

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
text: audit_summary() converts audit object created audit_panel() one-row tibble panel diagnostics.

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
code: audit_summary(x)

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
text: x object created audit_panel().

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
text: one-row tibble following columns: data Name audited object. id Name panel unit column. time Name time column. n_units Number unique panel units. n_periods Number unique time periods. observed_rows Number rows original data. observed_id_time_cells Number unique observed unit-time cells. expected_cells Number cells full unit--time grid. missing_cells Number missing unit-time cells. duplicate_cells Number duplicate unit-time cells. balanced Logical indicator whether panel balanced.

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
text: function useful users want compact, tabular summary panel audit. resulting tibble can printed, saved, joined metadata, combined across multiple datasets. summary includes number units, number time periods, observed rows, observed unit-time cells, expected unit-time cells, missing cells, duplicate cells, logical indicator whether panel balanced.

title: Summarize a panel audit — audit_summary
code: audit <- audit_panel(example_panel, id = id, time = year) audit_summary(audit) #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   data        id    time  n_units n_periods observed_rows observed_id_time_cells #>   <chr>       <chr> <chr>   <int>     <int>         <int>                  <int> #> 1 example_pa… id    year        3         4             9                      8 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: expected_cells <int>, missing_cells <int>, #> #   duplicate_cells <int>, balanced <lgl>

what: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
text: complete_panel() expands panel dataset every observed panel unit appears every observed time period. Newly created unit-time cells flagged audit columns, substantive variables left missing.

title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
code: complete_panel(data, id, time)

title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
text: data data frame tibble. id Unquoted column name identifying panel unit, person, firm, district, county, country. time Unquoted column name identifying time period, year, month, quarter, date.

title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
text: tibble containing completed panel grid. returned data include original columns plus following audit columns: unifyr_original_row Logical indicator rows present original data. unifyr_completed_cell Logical indicator rows created complete_panel(). unifyr_audit_action Character label describing whether row original added panel completion. returned tibble also includes attributes documenting panel identifier, time variable, number completed cells, audit note.

title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
text: function first audits panel using audit_panel(). duplicate unit-time cells present, function stops error. intentional: completing panel duplicate unit-time observations can produce ambiguous results. complete_panel() impute outcomes, covariates, treatment variables, substantive variables. creates missing unit-time rows implied full unit--time grid. Newly created rows flagged using audit columns.

title: Complete a panel dataset with an audit trail — complete_panel
code: panel_unique <- example_panel |>   dplyr::distinct(id, year, .keep_all = TRUE)  complete_panel(panel_unique, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 12 × 7 #>       id  year outcome treatment unifyr_original_row unifyr_completed_cell #>    <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> <lgl>               <lgl>                 #>  1     1  2020      10         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  2     1  2021      12         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  3     1  2022      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  4     1  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  5     2  2020      20         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  6     2  2021      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  7     2  2022      25         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #>  8     2  2023      NA        NA FALSE               TRUE                  #>  9     3  2020      30         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 10     3  2021      31         0 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 11     3  2022      32         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #> 12     3  2023      33         1 TRUE                FALSE                 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: unifyr_audit_action <chr>

title: Extract duplicate unit-time cells from a panel audit — duplicate_cells
text: Duplicate cells unit-time combinations appear original data. returned table includes count column n showing many rows present duplicated unit-time cell. function re-audit original dataset. simply extracts duplicate-cell table already stored audit object.

title: Extract duplicate unit-time cells from a panel audit — duplicate_cells
code: audit <- audit_panel(example_panel, id = id, time = year) duplicate_cells(audit) #>   id year n #> 1  1 2021 2

text: tibble one row per panel unit duplicate cells. output includes: unifyr_duplicate_cells Number duplicated unit-time cells unit. unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows Number extra rows caused duplicates. duplicates present, function returns units zero duplicate cells zero extra rows. tibble one row per panel unit duplicate cells. output includes: unifyr_duplicate_cells Number duplicated unit-time cells unit. unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows Number extra rows caused duplicates. duplicates present, function returns units zero duplicate cells zero extra rows.

text: function summarizes duplicate cells panel-unit level. useful users want identify units contribute duplicate unit-time observations. output reports number duplicated cells number extra rows implied duplicates. example, one unit-time cell appears three times, counts one duplicate cell two extra rows. function modify, drop, aggregate, impute data. function summarizes duplicate cells panel-unit level. useful users want identify units contribute duplicate unit-time observations. output reports number duplicated cells number extra rows implied duplicates. example, one unit-time cell appears three times, counts one duplicate cell two extra rows. function modify, drop, aggregate, impute data.

code: duplicate_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id unifyr_duplicate_cells unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows #>   <dbl>                  <int>                       <int> #> 1     1                      1                           1  duplicate_summary(example_panel, id = id, time = year) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>      id unifyr_duplicate_cells unifyr_duplicate_extra_rows #>   <dbl>                  <int>                       <int> #> 1     1                      1                           1

what: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
title: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
text: flag_panel_issues() adds row-level audit flags panel dataset. identifies duplicate unit-time observations preserving original data structure.

title: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
code: flag_panel_issues(data, id, time)

title: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
text: data data frame tibble. id Unquoted column name identifying panel unit, person, firm, district, county, country. time Unquoted column name identifying time period, year, month, quarter, date.

title: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
text: tibble containing original data plus row-level audit columns: unifyr_row_id Integer row identifier based original row order. unifyr_id_time_n Number rows unit-time combination. unifyr_duplicate_cell Logical indicator rows belong duplicate unit-time cell. returned tibble also includes attributes documenting panel identifier, time variable, audit note.

title: Flag row-level panel data issues — flag_panel_issues
text: function useful users want inspect problematic rows directly rather receiving summary table. adds diagnostic columns indicate many times unit-time cell appears whether row part duplicate cell. flag_panel_issues() add rows, remove rows, complete panel, impute missing values.

title: Identify missing unit-time cells — panel_gaps

what: unifyr: Post-import tools for analysis-ready panel data — unifyr-package
title: unifyr: Post-import tools for analysis-ready panel data — unifyr-package
text: unifyr provides tidyverse-friendly tools auditing, diagnosing, documenting panel datasets import. package focuses transparent panel structure checks, gap detection, duplicate identification, reproducible audit trails empirical research workflows.

title: unifyr: Post-import tools for analysis-ready panel data — unifyr-package
text: package designed help researchers identify common panel-data problems estimation, including missing id-time cells, duplicate id-time cells, incomplete panel structure. Functions unifyr silently impute, aggregate, drop observations.

title: unifyr: Post-import tools for analysis-ready panel data — unifyr-package
text: Maintainer: Lavanya Desiraju desirajulavanya@gmail.com

title: unifyr 0.1.0
text: Initial development release unifyr. Added audit_panel() summarizing panel structure, balance, missing cells, duplicate unit-time observations. Added duplicate_summary() identifying duplicate unit-time cells. Added gap_summary() summarizing missing time periods panel unit. Added flag_panel_issues() row-level panel diagnostics. Added complete_panel() creating complete unit-time grids without imputing observed variables. Added README examples package documentation.
